content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 29, 1988

Stephanie Brown
Manatt, Phelps, Rothenberg
& Evans
1200 New Hampshire Ave., N.W., Suite 200
Washington, D.C. 20036

Re: Our File No. G-88-244

Dear Ms. Brown:

You have requested copies of statutes, regulations, forms
and other documents relating to elections laws in California.
We have enclosed the following documents.

(1) A copy of the Political Reform Act as amended to
January 1, 1988.

(2) Copies of Propositions 68 and 73, which were both
passed by the voters June 7, 1988.

(3) 1988 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act.

(4) 1988 Information Manual for Lobbying Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act.

(5) A complete set of 1988 campaign and lobbying
forms.

(6) A copy of the Commission's June 1988 Bulletin.
As you requested, we have added your firm to our Bulletin
mailing list.

All regulations, opinions and enforcement decisions of the
Fair Political Practices Commission are published by California
Continuing Education of the Bar and may be obtained through
subscription with that organization. For further information,
please contact:

Julie Lyon
Continuing Education of the Bar
2300 Shattuck Avenue
Berkeley, CA 94704

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Stephanie Brown
June 29, 1988
Page 2


     You also can obtain a complete set of Commission
regulations (Division 6, Title 2, California Code of
Regulations, Section 18110 et seq.) through the California
Department of General Services.  For further information,
please contact:

               Department of General Services
               Documents Section
               P.O. Box 1015
               North Highlands, CA  95660
               (916) 924-4800

     We are unable to provide copies of California statutes
other than the Political Reform Act.  However, West Publishing
Company and Bancroft-Whitney (Deerings) have subscription
services for the California codes.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Kathryn E. Donovan
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:KED:plh

Enclosures

MANATT, PHELPS, ROTHENBERG & EVANS
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

1200 NEW HAMPSHIRE AVENUE, N.W.
SUITE 200
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20036

LOS ANGELES
1355 WEST OLYMPIC BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90064
(213) 312-4000

TELEPHONE (202) 463-4300

June 22, 1988


Greg W. Baugher
Executive Director
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Baugher:

     We are updating our files to reflect any changes which may have
been made in the election and ethics law sections of your state code.
To assist us in this effort, we would like to request the following
materials from your office:

     (1) Election Law Statutes
     (2) Regulations promulgated by your office
     (3) Publications provided by your office for:
          (a) Candidates
          (b) Political Action Committees
          (c) Corporations
     (4) Limitations on PAC and corporate contributions
     (5) Index of pertinent advisory opinions
          (Attorney General and Secretary of State)
     (6) Registration, reporting and disclosures forms for political
          action committees and corporations
     (7) Ethics Laws, Regulations, Advisory Opinions, and Financial
          Disclosure Forms
     (8) Lobbying Rules & Regulations
     (9) Any information or compilations of local city or county
          election, ethics or lobbying laws in your state

     Additionally, we would like to be placed on your mailing list
for any future publications or materials you might be providing. If
there is a charge for these or future materials, please advise by
return mail or contact Stephanie Brown in this office.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [signature]
                                   Stephanie A. Brown

[illegible] bicycle was a
letter recently.
please add this
to the file.
Thanks ed

[State seal]

'AY DAVIS
the State of California
BOX 942850
O, CA 94250-0001

445-7941
July 1, 1988


Stephanie A. Brown
c/o MANATT, PHELPS, ROTHENBERG
& EVANS
Attorneys at Law
1200 New Hampshire Avenue, N.W.
Suite 200
Washington, D.C.  20036

Dear Ms. Brown:

We have received your letter dated June 22, 1988, requesting
copies of various election laws, regulations, etc. The type of
information you are requesting is maintained by the California
Secretary of State and the Fair Political Practices Commission,
rather than the State Controller. Therefore, we are taking the
liberty of referring copies of your letter to those agencies.

We hope this will be of assistance to you.

Very truly yours,

GRAY DAVIS, State Controller

By ___________________
    D. Robert Shuman
    Chief Counsel

cc: Secretary of State
    FPPC
    Debbie Edgecomb

MANATT, PHELPS, ROTHENBERG & EVANS
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

1200 NEW HAMPSHIRE AVENUE, N.W.
SUITE 200
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20036

TELEPHONE (202) 463-4300

LOS ANGELES
11355 WEST OLYMPIC BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90064
(213) 312-4000

June 22, 1988

Gray Davis
State Controller
300 Capitol Mall
P.O. Box 942850
Sacramento, California 94285

Dear Mr. Davis:

We are updating our files to reflect any changes which may have
been made in the election and ethics law sections of your state code.
To assist us in this effort, we would like to request the following
materials from your office:

(1) Election Law Statutes
(2) Regulations promulgated by your office
(3) Publications provided by your office for:
    (a) Candidates
    (b) Political Action Committees
    (c) Corporations
(4) Limitations on PAC and corporate contributions
(5) Index of pertinent advisory opinions
    (Attorney General and Secretary of State)
(6) Registration, reporting and disclosures forms for political
    action committees and corporations
(7) Ethics Laws, Regulations, Advisory Opinions, and Financial
    Disclosure Forms
(8) Lobbying Rules & Regulations
(9) Any information or compilations of local city or county
    election, ethics or lobbying laws in your state

Additionally, we would like to be placed on your mailing list
for any future publications or materials you might be providing. If
there is a charge for these or future materials, please advise by
return mail or contact Stephanie Brown in this office.

Sincerely,

Stephanie A. Brown

[illegible]

sections.question_synthetic: A law firm requests copies of California election laws, regulations, forms, and other documents relating to campaign disclosure, lobbying regulations, and ethics laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided requested documents including the Political Reform Act, Propositions 68 and 73, 1988 Information Manuals, campaign and lobbying forms, and the June 1988 Bulletin, along with information on how to obtain additional regulations and statutes through other sources.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a three-part document collection: incoming request letter from law firm to FPPC (June 22), referral letter from State Controller to FPPC (June 27), and FPPC response letter from General Counsel (June 29). No formal Q&A section; informational/administrative correspondence. Extracted from FPPC response letter as primary document.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A law firm requests copies of California election laws, regulations, forms, and other documents relating to campaign disclosure, lobbying regulations, and ethics laws.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provided requested documents including the Political Reform Act, Propositions 68 and 73, 1988 Information Manuals, campaign and lobbying forms, and the June 1988 Bulletin, along with information on how to obtain additional regulations and statutes through other sources.

embedding.summary: Manatt, Phelps & Rothenberg law firm requested copies of California election and ethics law materials. The FPPC General Counsel responded by providing the requested documents and directing the firm to appropriate sources for additional regulations and statutes.
